# Execute query

**Connector:** [SDP - Query](../README.md) (`sdp-query`) · **Action index:** [all actions](README.md)
**Endpoint:** `POST /api/v3/reports/_execute_query` · **Operation ID:** `ExecuteQuery`

Runs **one read-only SQL SELECT** against the SDP database via the Execute Query report API and returns the rows. One good SELECT (JOINs, aggregates, custom fields) replaces many REST GET calls.

## Parameters

| Designer label | Name | Where | Type | Required |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Input Data (JSON) | `input_data` | form field | string | yes |
| Accept | `Accept` | header | string | yes |

### Input Data (JSON) (`input_data`)

The `{"query":"SELECT ..."}` envelope — formats and variations below.

### Accept (`Accept`)

Fixed technical header, preset to `application/vnd.manageengine.sdp.v3+json` — it selects
SDP's V3 media type (and, for worklogs, the current V3 worklog format). It is marked
*internal*, so the designer hides it from makers; you'll only see it in the connector's code
view. Leave it as is, including `required: true` — Power Platform silently drops optional
internal headers (see the repo's [LESSONS.md](../../../LESSONS.md)).

## The `input_data` payload — formats and variations

This action sends `input_data` as a **form field** (`application/x-www-form-urlencoded`
body) — the connector does the encoding, you paste plain JSON, exactly like the query-string
variant on the list actions.

The envelope has exactly one key — `query` — whose value is the SQL string.

### Formatting the SQL inside JSON

- **Alias every column with `AS`** — the response rows are keyed by the aliases; un-aliased
  expressions get unstable names.
- Keep the SQL on **one line** (or escape newlines as `\n`); never leave raw line breaks
  inside the JSON string.
- SQL string literals use **single quotes**, which need no escaping in JSON — convenient. If
  you must embed a double quote (identifier quoting), escape it as `\"`.

### Variation 1 — the capability probe (run this once at setup)

```json
{"query":"SELECT 1 AS test"}
```

If it errors, Execute Query is not available on this connection (permission / edition /
build) — fall back to the REST connectors.

### Variation 2 — recent requests with status (live-verified template 01)

```json
{"query":"SELECT wo.workorderid AS id, wo.title AS subject, sd.statusname AS status, wo.createdtime AS created_epoch_ms FROM workorder wo LEFT JOIN workorderstates ws ON wo.workorderid = ws.workorderid LEFT JOIN statusdefinition sd ON ws.statusid = sd.statusid ORDER BY wo.workorderid DESC LIMIT 20"}
```

### Variation 3 — aggregate: request count by status (template 02)

```json
{"query":"SELECT sd.statusname AS status, COUNT(*) AS total FROM workorder wo LEFT JOIN workorderstates ws ON wo.workorderid = ws.workorderid LEFT JOIN statusdefinition sd ON ws.statusid = sd.statusid GROUP BY sd.statusname ORDER BY total DESC"}
```

### Variation 4 — cross-module JOIN: requests to assets (template 07)

```json
{"query":"SELECT wta.workorderid AS request_id, wo.title AS subject, r.resourcename AS asset_name FROM workordertoasset wta JOIN workorder wo ON wta.workorderid = wo.workorderid JOIN resources r ON wta.assetid = r.resourceid ORDER BY wta.workorderid DESC LIMIT 25"}
```

All eight starter queries live in [`templates/`](../../../templates/) (build-14990 schema,
each verified to run live). **Physical table/column names are build-specific** — keep SQL in
flow variables or the templates library, not hard-baked, so an upgrade can't strand you.

### Formatting rules

- **One line of valid JSON.** Paste the whole envelope as a single line. JSON syntax only:
  **double quotes** around every key and string value, no trailing commas, no comments.
- **IDs are strings.** SDP returns its numeric IDs as JSON strings; send them the same way —
  `{"id":"1234"}`.
- **Reference fields take an object**, addressed by any unique key you have:
  `{"name":"High"}`, `{"id":"301"}`, or — for people — `{"email_id":"tech@company.com"}`.
- **Dates and times are epoch milliseconds (UTC).** Payload date fields are objects like
  `{"value":"1735689600000"}` (2025-01-01T00:00:00Z); inside `search_criteria` the value is the
  bare digit string.
- **Building the JSON in a flow:** dynamic content is spliced in as plain text, so the result
  must still be valid JSON. For free text that may contain quotes/newlines, sanitize first
  (e.g. `replace(<text>, '"', '\"')` after doubling backslashes), or assemble the payload with
  a **Compose**/`json()` step and `string()` it into the parameter.

## Authentication

Nothing to do per action: the connection you created for this connector sends the technician
API key on every call as the `authtoken` header (a secured connection parameter — it never
lives in a flow or in the definition). Generate keys in SDP under *Admin → Technicians →
Generate API Key*, ideally for a least-privilege integration technician. The repo
[README](../../../README.md) covers key scoping and the HTTPS-only rule.

## Response

Success (200) returns the `ExecuteQueryEnvelope` shape:

- `response_status` — ResponseStatus object
- `execute_query` — object — Execute Query

`execute_query.data` is an array of row objects **keyed by your SQL aliases** (hence the `AS` rule). Empty result = empty array, still status_code 2000.

Success means `response_status.status_code` **2000** — check that, not just HTTP 200. One
shape wobble to handle in flows: on **list** successes `response_status` is an **array** of
status objects, on **single-resource and error** responses it's an **object**. The connector
deliberately leaves it untyped; branch on `Array.isArray`-style logic (e.g. Power Automate
`first(...)` when it's an array).

Anything else comes back on the `default` response as the SDP error envelope — surface `status_code` and `messages` in your flow's failure branch.

## Notes, limits & verification

- Live-verified end to end on the demo (SELECT works, DML/catalog/GET rejections confirmed).
- **SELECT only** — `UPDATE`/`DELETE`/`INSERT` are rejected server-side (4004), as are `information_schema`/catalog queries. **POST only** — a GET returns 4021 *Invalid HTTP method*. All verified live.
- Needs an **admin-scoped key** (*Create Query Report* permission), and the endpoint is edition/build-gated on some instances — always run the probe first.
- Prefer fixed, vetted query templates over exposing a free-form SQL box to end users; see the [connector README](../README.md#security-note).
